items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center expansion receives a building permit at 131 Great Oaks Bl. The project will add server and networking infrastructure to support cloud computing and digital services in San José.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A demolition and renovation permitted on Blossom Hill
items.summary: A $810,000 demolition and addition permit issued for the Chick-fil-A at 1162 Blossom Hill Rd. The project will rebuild and expand the restaurant location.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument art acquisition agreement approved
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a Seeds of Peace Monument artwork by artist Mario Chiodo at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The work commemorates peace and will become a permanent public installation in the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment, determining funding levels for the district's operations and improvements in the coming year.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed an update to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015–2040). The General Plan guides land use, development, and public services; updates to this element address evolving health and safety priorities for the city.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the leadership role responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council policy.

items.headline: City releases draft water shortage and master plans for public input
items.summary: Sunnyvale's Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. Residents can comment on how the city plans to manage water supply and respond to shortages.

items.headline: Spring grants available for community events and neighborhood projects
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood improvements.
